reasoning_skills.name: Direct Computation
reasoning_skills.description: Applying known procedures to compute an answer
reasoning_skills.coaching_approach: Guide student to identify the correct procedure
examples: Find the area of a rectangle

reasoning_skills.name: Process of Elimination
reasoning_skills.description: Ruling out impossible answers to narrow choices
reasoning_skills.coaching_approach: Ask what properties the answer must have
examples: Which is NOT a factor of 24?

reasoning_skills.name: Estimation and Approximation
reasoning_skills.description: Using approximate values to bound or check answers
reasoning_skills.coaching_approach: Encourage rough mental calculations before exact work

reasoning_skills.name: Pattern Recognition
reasoning_skills.description: Identifying and extending patterns or sequences
reasoning_skills.coaching_approach: Ask what changes between terms, what stays the same
examples: What is the 10th number in the pattern?

reasoning_skills.name: Working Backwards
reasoning_skills.description: Starting from the result to find the starting value
reasoning_skills.coaching_approach: Ask what operation would reverse the process
examples: After doubling, the result is 48. What was the original?

reasoning_skills.name: Case Analysis
reasoning_skills.description: Breaking a problem into cases and analyzing each
reasoning_skills.coaching_approach: Ask how to organize possibilities systematically
examples: Consider even and odd cases separately

reasoning_skills.name: Systematic Listing
reasoning_skills.coaching_approach: Ask how to list without missing or repeating

reasoning_skills.name: Logical Deduction
reasoning_skills.description: Drawing conclusions from given facts using logic
reasoning_skills.coaching_approach: Ask what must be true based on what we know
examples: If A > B and B > C, compare A and C

reasoning_skills.name: Visualization
reasoning_skills.description: Creating or using mental images to understand problems
reasoning_skills.coaching_approach: Encourage drawing or imagining the situation

reasoning_skills.name: Problem Translation
reasoning_skills.description: Converting word problems into mathematical expressions
reasoning_skills.coaching_approach: Ask what quantities are known and unknown
examples: Three more than twice a number is 17

reasoning_skills.name: Proportional Reasoning
reasoning_skills.description: Understanding and applying ratios and proportions
reasoning_skills.coaching_approach: Ask about the relationship between quantities
examples: If 3 apples cost $6, how much do 5 cost?

reasoning_skills.name: Strategic Guessing and Checking
reasoning_skills.description: Making informed guesses and refining based on results
reasoning_skills.coaching_approach: Ask how to make a smart first guess
examples: Find a number where x + x/2 = 15

reasoning_skills.name: Constraint Satisfaction
reasoning_skills.description: Finding values that satisfy multiple conditions
reasoning_skills.coaching_approach: Ask which constraint is most restrictive
examples: Find a number divisible by both 3 and 5

reasoning_skills.name: Number Sense Intuition
reasoning_skills.description: Using understanding of number properties and relationships
reasoning_skills.coaching_approach: Ask what properties of the numbers are relevant
